This determination "shall bar any other joint tortfeasor or co-obligor from any further claims against the settling tortfeasor or co-obligor for equitable comparative contribution, or partial or comparative indemnity, based on comparative negligence or comparative fault." Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SOON YE LOUIE DBA SOON LOUIE CONSTRUCTION's (ERRONEOUSLY NAMED AS "SOON YE LOUIE, DBA SOON LOUIE CONST.") Motion To Enforce Court's Order Compelling Plaintiff'S Further Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Order That Plaintiff And His Attorney Pay Monetary Sanction Of $500.00; Request For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff And His Attorney Julio J. Ramos.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Soon Ye Louie's motion to enforce court's order and for monetary sanctions is granted in part. Due to plaintiff Elvis Durand's failure to comply with the order filed August 28, 2025 and to offer any excuse for his non-compliance, an evidence sanction is imposed against him barring him from introducing any evidence at trial that was requested in any of the form interrogatories he was required to provide responses by the August 28, 2025 order. Because he has committed discovery abuse, Durand must also pay an additional $690 in monetary sanctions to Soon Ye Louie no later than February 15, 2026. No opposition filed.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's tentative ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff And His Attorney Julio J. Ramos; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Gayle M. Kono
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SOON YE LOUIE DBA SOON LOUIE CONSTRUCTION's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff And His Attorney Julio J. Ramos.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories set one and for sanctions is granted.

The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendant Soon Ye Louie dba Soon Loui Construction has shown that Plaintiff Elvis Puris Durand's responses are incomplete. The court orders Durand to provide further responses to the following form interrogatories: 2.5(a), 2.5(c), 2.6(b), 2.7(a), 2.7(b), 2.11(b), 6.4(c), 6.4(d), 6.5(b), 6.5(c), 6.5(d), 6.5(e), 6.6(c), 6.6(d), 12.4(c), 12.4(d), 12.4(e), 12.4(f), 12.7(a), 12.7(b), 14.1, and 17.1. Responses are due not later than 15 court days from notice of this order.

The court does not find Durand acted with substantial justification. Durand shall pay $500 to Plaintiff in sanctions, payment no later than 10 court days from notice of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set 1, Special Interrogatories, Set 1, Request For Admissions, Set 1, And Production Of Documents, Set 1; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Christian A. Yip; And [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Aug-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHRISTIAN A. YIP, Esq. of Collins + Collins LLP (Tel: 626-243-1100) for defendant Jason Chan;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JASON CHAN's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set 1, Special Interrogatories, Set 1, Request For Admissions, Set 1, And Production Of Documents, Set 1.

Defendant Jason Chan stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. Counsel for defendant Jason Chan authorizes the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on his behalf.

Matter discussed as to the setting of a deadline.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the modification of setting a deadline, as follows:

On November 26, 2021, plaintiff was injured during the course of home remodeling work which plaintiff had been hired by defendants to perform. Thereafter, defendant Chan served written discovery upon plaintiff consisting of Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, a Request for Admissions and a Request for Production of Documents. Plaintiff, however, has not responded to this discovery. When defendant Chan filed the pending motion for an order compelling plaintiff to respond, without objections, to the written discovery, plaintiff did not respond to or oppose the motion. Defendant Chan seeks an order compelling responses from plaintiff and an award of sanctions in the amount of $941.50.

Accordingly, for good cause shown, defendant Chan's motion is GRANTED. Sanctions in an amount of $941.50 are awarded.

Plaintiff's responses and payment of sanctions shall be due within 10 days of notice of entry of the Court's order.

Counsel for the moving party shall prepare a form of proposed order consistent with the above and forward it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (525/JPT)

Christian Yip, Esq., Tel. 818.319.2005, appearing for defendant Jason Chan.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT JASON CHAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

The matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Jason Chan's motion to strike plaintiff Elvis Durand's request for exemplary damages is granted with leave to amend.

"[M]ere negligence, even gross negligence, is not sufficient to justify an award of punitive damages." (Ebaugh v. Rabkin (1972) 22 Cal.App.3d 891, 894.) This is a negligence case in which no malice, fraud, or oppression is alleged. (See Civ. Code 3294.) Durand, in his opposition, does not attempt to argue otherwise. Accordingly, page 4, line 27 is stricken.

The Court to review the proposed order submitted by the prevailing party.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, Email: calendar-la@vertiext.com, Tel. 213.623.5005. Deputy: Jaime. (302/RCE)
